meta.title: AgentLease Unified Access Control
meta.description: A unified Lev design for RBAC, scoped permissions, auth-material, data/PII leases, AgentPing approvals, and runtime enforcement.

props.title: AgentLease Unified Access Control
props.subtitle: One runtime grant model for RBAC, scoped permissions, auth-material, data/PII leases, AgentPing approvals, and enforceable receipts.

props.content: Lev should treat `AgentLeaseGrant` as the active runtime authority. RBAC, ABAC, scoped permissions, and human approvals become admission inputs; Exec, Harness, Vault, browser adapters, worker lifecycles, and mesh projections enforce active grants and emit receipts.

props.label: Core Decision
props.title: Lease as the narrow waist
props.subtitle: The important split is between policy inputs and authority-bearing grants.

props.title: Recommended: Unified AgentLeaseGrant
props.content: Use one grant lifecycle: `PolicyRequest -> AccessDecision -> AgentPing approval -> AgentLeaseGrant -> EnforcementDescriptor -> AgentLeaseUse -> Receipt`. This gives one audit path for tools, files, secrets, PII, browser control, workers, mutable state, budgets, and mesh delegation.

props.title: Rejected: Parallel authority systems
props.content: Keeping RBAC, scoped permissions, PII, vault, browser, and worker leases separate looks faster, but it fragments policy and makes hidden bypasses likely. RBAC-first is familiar, but ambient role authority does not fit time-bounded agent execution.

flowchart TD
  A[FlowMind / config] -->|required leases + TTL defaults| B[AgentGuard / runtime governance]
  B -->|deny with reasons| C[Denied]
  B -->|allow| D[AgentLeaseGrant]
  B -->|require approval| E[AgentPing approval surface]
  E -->|structured human response| B
  D --> F[EnforcementDescriptor]
  F --> G[Exec / Harness / Vault / Browser / Worker]
  G --> H[AgentLeaseUse]
  H --> I[Receipt / GateProof / AuditEvent]
  I --> J[Mesh projection: assertions, digests, receipts only]
props.caption: Policy stays upstream; runtime sees enforceable descriptors; evidence flows downstream.

props.title: AgentPing is the human-loop surface, not the policy engine
props.content: AgentPing renders requests and captures responses. AgentGuard/runtime governance issues or denies grants. Runtime adapters enforce descriptors. This preserves the existing AgentPing lease UX without letting a browser-specific implementation become the general access model.

props.label: Resource Policy
props.title: One lease schema, class-specific enforcement
props.subtitle: Sensitive classes default to brokered use, redacted views, derived answers, or brokered actions.

props.title: core/domain
props.content: Pure `AgentLease` nouns, validators, class policies, and receipt reference types.

props.title: AgentGuard / runtime governance
props.content: RBAC, ABAC, scoped permission, material policy, approval routing, and final grant issuance.

props.title: AgentPing
props.content: Lease request rendering, structured human response, approval timeouts, and review UX.

props.title: Exec / Harness
props.content: Consume enforcement descriptors, check active grants, run work, and emit use receipts.

props.title: Vault / broker
props.content: Resolve sensitive material behind key names and broker descriptors; never default to agent-visible plaintext.

props.title: Mesh
props.content: Exchange assertions, digests, capability advertisements, and receipts. No raw auth-material, raw PII, live handles, or reusable bearer leases.

props.title: Bridge, don't replace, current evidence
props.content: The first proposal should bridge existing FlowMind required leases, runtime-governance decisions, AgentPing browser approval flows, vault depth metadata, warm-worker leases, and mesh auth-material guards into one domain contract.

props.label: Fitness Functions
props.title: Checks that keep the architecture honest

rows.reason: Secrets and PII must not appear in pings, receipts, logs, or mesh envelopes.
Time-bounded authority is the core promise.
A grant cannot become ambient transferable authority.
The unified schema must not become a bag of strings.
AgentPing is UX and response capture, not policy authority.

rows.threshold: 0 raw material or bearer lease fields
rows.reason: Mesh route trust must not become secret replication.

props.title: 2. Policy bridge
props.content: Map current runtime-governance decisions and FlowMind `required_leases` into `AgentLeaseRequest`.

props.title: 3. AgentPing request shape
props.content: Render non-browser lease classes without giving AgentPing final grant authority.

props.title: 4. Broker descriptors
props.content: Add descriptor shape for `secret`, `pii`, `network`, and sensitive brokered action paths.

props.title: 5. Leakage tests
props.content: Prove no raw secret or PII values can enter Run, Receipt, GateProof, AgentPing payloads, request logs, or mesh envelopes.
